attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00240 — SEQ derived from source note CIA-040; maintainer/ingest --reseq to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket.
Age inferred as 34 (adult) — the source note gives no age; demographics.age is schema-required so a plausible adult value was supplied. Please confirm on clinician review.
Sex inferred as female from leg-shaving + workout leggings context; not explicitly stated in the source. Please confirm on clinician review.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person home assessment. The follicle-centred pustular pattern and lesion distribution are treated as video-observable in 01.objective_data_offered (verified:false); clinician palpation excluding deep abscess/fluctuance, regional lymph-node palpation, and measured vitals are sealed in 10.telehealth_limits_for_this_case / 11 as clinician-only.

Tier calibrated to true acuity (T2 baseline, min-viable T1): a benign, afebrile, self-limiting superficial folliculitis. The source note's 'urgent after-hours MBS 585-600 / aggressive spreading staph / early cellulitis' framing is billing/anxiety language and was NOT used to floor the tier; over-triage to ED/000 is encoded as a commission error.
Doses taken verbatim from the source note (chlorhexidine 4% wash BD; mupirocin 2% BD for 5 days) — none synthesised. Adult case, so no paediatric-dosing concern.
AMT/PBS medication codes not assigned — omitted rather than guessed (only diagnosis/differential SNOMED + ICD-10-AM candidates registered); all codes are unverified_pending_terminology_receipt.

SNOMED/ICD-10-AM codes are best-effort candidates from model knowledge (folliculitis 13600006/L73.9; cellulitis 128045006; furuncle 12377003; herpes simplex 88594005) — pending terminology-server verification.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
